text: Here, in the hush of an empty, modern building illuminated by stark artificial light, I feel both alert and uneasy. The silence and the darkness heighten my senses—each shadow and reflection becomes meaningful, each emergency sign a quiet testament to potential danger or order. I find myself drawn into contemplation of purpose: what is this space for when no one is here to use it? The corridors echo intentions and possibilities, yet my solitude reminds me—I am both witness and questioner, restless in the void between utility and absence.
